characters.name: Dale Armature
characters.description: - "School Pageant" Dale is an eccentric and fame-obsessed musician. A character tic of his is plucking at his brow. He's nervous and seems to be insecure about his writing.

characters.name: Bartholomew
characters.description: Gender Bartholomew had a playful and affectionate temperament, and he quickly trusted Orel. He was also unusually intelligent for a small stray dog, doing stock tricks and reenacting Bible scenes on command. He carried no animosity toward other animals or people, regardless if they liked him in return.

characters.name: Nurse Bendy
characters.description: - "Alone" In most settings, Nurse Bendy appears to be little more than a stereotypical "dumb blonde." She finds no satisfaction in working at the hospital, nor in having sex withPrincipal Fakey. Whether she's followingDr. Potterswheel's demands or tending to patients herself, she's usually depicted as incompetent at her job and possessing no desire to improve. At home, she's almost an entirely different character: she's excited to be a responsible, doting mother for her "family" of teddy bears. In order to cope with trauma, Nurse Bendyage regressesto a childlike state. Through playing house with her stuffed animals, she fulfills her dream of being a great mom with an unconditionally loving family. Even if she doesn't take her medical work seriously, Nurse Bendy wants to set a good example for her son (first a teddy bear named "Sonny," later Joe). Yet despite living in her dream house, Nurse Bendy is still on edge that her "Hubby" bear might take advantage of her just like other men have; when he falls on her in "Alone" in a suggestive way, she immediately panics and later ties him to his chair. Until she reunited with Joe, Nurse Bendy tried to compensate for her lacking self-image by having her stuffed animals look up to her by default. As she bonded with her real son, who loved her as she was, Nurse Bendy gradually rid herself of her stuffed animal substitutes and focused on raising Joe.

characters.name: Mr. Carvedson
characters.description: This article needs to becleaned up, whether by grammar, spelling, or content!   He's as pious and sensible as the other adults in Moralton. His survival tips range from fundamentalist nonsense to common knowledge that was later disproven, namely drinking urine to conserve energy.

characters.name: Francis Clara Censordoll
characters.description: -"Alone" Ms. Censordoll is an egotistical and self-righteous woman who plans to rule over Moralton someday. She is often seen picketing or burning anything that she deems unholy, ranging fromThe Wizard of Ozto hors d'oevres. Despite the nature of her chosen issues, Censordoll is intelligent and can manipulate others to enforce her views. She has an affinity for eggs, specifically the undercooked and slithery ones. This stems from her infancy, when her mother removed her reproductive parts. She also loves using fire to "cleanse" any impurities from her library. Despite championing for the big man upstairs, Censordoll is willing to use whatever sinful means necessary to achieve her goals, including manipulation and adultery. She prefers to use religion to keepMoralton's citizens in line more than anything else.

characters.name: Dr. Chosenberg
characters.description: - "Holy Visage" Dr. Chosenberg is one of the few rational people that appear in the show, rightfully skeptical ofMoralton's religious practices. Despite his criticism of the adults of Moralton, he's patient withOrel.

characters.description: Gender Junior has a decent relationship with his family and likes to play games with his sister. He's also passionate about singing. His grades are normally A+ or A, meaning that he is smart and possibly gifted.

characters.description: -"God's Chef" Clicky is abrasive and to the point, driven by making sure everything in school is clean and orderly. Despite being hard to approach, Clicky sometimes offers advice.

characters.name: Cecil Creepler
characters.description: -"Courtship" While Mr. Creepler sold frozen treats to the children of Moralton around the clock, he didn't care for them outside of sexual attraction.Doughy Latchkeywas a favorite of his. Mr. Creepler would often attempt to lure him into his van through gifts, compliments and free ice cream. Not only would he try to rape children, but he would do the same to women, especially black haired women. "Raped" would reveal that he did this to have children of his own, and when Ms. Sculptham aborted one of them, he would fall into a deep depression.

characters.name: Principal Fakey
characters.description: - "Love" Principal Fakey is a selfish, pleasure-driven man with little respect for the children he manages. He is so frugal that he refuses to spend money on qualified teachers when needed, and is responsible for many free thinkers getting poor grades and worse treatment. While he bears guilt for his affair withNurse Bendy, he's not above lying to his wife in order to get another fix. He also considers it no fault of his own. As you might have guessed, he is a misogynist.

characters.name: Sal Figurelli
characters.description: -Catchphrase Mr. Figurelli is a comically, brazenly corrupt businessman who isn't abovebriberyand usingchild labor. While he's usually happy-go-lucky and carefree, Sal and his wife's arguments can become heated, to the point where they almost divorced. Sal appreciatesOrelandDoughyin some way, as he occasionally treats them with frozen strawberry phosphates. He is considered both in the show and out as an Italian stereotype. He is also antisemitic.

characters.name: Flygron
characters.description: - "Satan" Flygron is an amicable, gluttonous and pleasure-seeking man. Unlike what you'd expect from a Satanist, he's tolerant of all walks of life, excluding Christians and pedophiles. His rituals feature a smorgasbord of food and sensuality for all consenting parties, since his religion interprets Satan as an amalgamation of selfish sensations instead of a deity. He has a verbal tic of ending his sentences with "and what not." When talking over the phone, he alters his voice to sound more intimidating.

characters.name: Gladys Foamwire
characters.description: Gender Gladys was an obsessive stalker, and a religious nut even forMoralton's standards. Stephanie's recollections of her imply that she wasn't a great mother, either.

characters.name: Dolly Forghetty
characters.description: Gender Dolly is a reasonable authority figure who seems to be familiar with everyone in Moralton, as most of the residents frequent her bar. She likes to stoke up conversations and is patient with her patrons. Such friendliness often has the tendency to turn into flirting despite her not wanting to actually get with any of her patrons. She has her limits, though, as Clay's misogynistic tirade in "Sacrifice" was so bad that she quit mid-day. Dolly goes to church sometimes, and, as evidenced by "Pleasure," she has a self-flagellation fetish.

Dolly goes to church sometimes, and, as evidenced by "Pleasure," she has a self-flagellation fetish.

characters.name: Walt Gluegun
